items.rationale: His signature thesis: crypto networks that form consensus on physical state (telecom, energy, transport) and enable user-owned, user-operated physical businesses. He frames it as the future of consumer crypto where users interact with chains unknowingly.

Believes verifiable compute is a new, still-underhyped developer primitive. Excited by zkVMs like Jolt enabling devs to experiment, plus applications in trustless ML, private compute, and integrity proofs.

Argues Ethereum cannot reach endgame scalability on native DA alone (~9166 TPS today) and that non-native DA layers (EigenDA, Celestia, Espresso) targeting ~1GB/s are required short-term to scale the rollup roadmap.

Views L2s as 'implicit sharding' that lets anyone deploy a shard, with shared sequencing, proof aggregation and restaking forming the cross-shard coordination layer. Excited shared sequencers could deliver the first decentralized sequencer and atomic composability.

Surprised more DeFi protocols don't use ZK co-processors to run complex pricing functions and risk modeling off-chain proven trustlessly on-chain, lowering gas while improving risk management on compute-limited chains.
Calls restaking 'the ultimate nerdsnipe' and is very optimistic about EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment; also cites restaking/mesh security as part of the rollup coordination layer.
items.age_note: Strongest single statement is 2024-02-22 (~4 months old) but reinforced via restaking references in June 2024 rollup-coordination posts, so current.

items.rationale: Cypherpunk-driven; advocates 'defense in depth' for crypto—proofs for integrity, enclaves for privacy—and promotes mixnets and private payments as routes to social/political change.
Sees blockchains + ZK as uniquely enabling user-owned censorship-resistant AI companions and trustless proving of simple ML models (linear regression, small NNs—not LLMs yet).
A DePIN sub-thesis he is actively meme-branding: token-incentivized decentralized energy grids (and transportation/wireless) as physical-network applications of crypto.
items.age_note: Coined/pushed April 2024 (2024-04-16/18), not explicitly restated by 2024-06-30, but consistent with his ongoing DePIN energy framing.

items.rationale: Long-standing interest in tokens as high-dimensional 'value vectors' and mechanism design for coordination beyond finance—but his most recent framing demotes the token from product to mere tool for incentive problems.
items.age_note: Earlier (2023-04-21) he heavily promoted 'Tokenology'; by 2024-06-26/27 he reframes: the token is not the product and tokenomics is not protocol design. Excitement persists but is now subordinated to protocol/product design.

items.rationale: Believes the field under-invests in infrastructure relative to apps, and that building the rails for human coordination is a massive opportunity—consistent with his network-over-library, build-a-network mantra.
items.age_note: Core statement is 2023-07-25 (~11 months old); reinforced by 2024-06-18 'If you're building in crypto, build a network.'

items.rationale: Core ideological worry: centralized protocols 'create hard times,' and centrally controlled hardware undermines user ownership. Decentralized verification is needed to make networks genuinely user-owned and resist capture.
Considers MEV a poor catch-all that conflates user-harming extraction with neutral protocol design, and frames eliminating exploitative information-asymmetry value extraction as an open problem.
items.age_note: Main critique dates to 2023-05-12 (>1 year old), only lightly echoed via jokes in 2024 (e.g. 2024-04-10); treat as persisted, lower weight.

items.rationale: While bullish that non-native DA is needed to scale Ethereum, he explicitly flags that it introduces additional security assumptions—a caveat to his own DA thesis.

Sarcastically frames regulation as protecting incumbents from decentralized competition; sees clear pro-crypto legislation (FIT 21) as a win, implying concern about the prevailing hostile/capture regime.
items.age_note: Sarcastic 'regulatory capture' line is 2023-10-26; supported by positive FIT 21 reaction 2024-05-22 indicating ongoing concern about US regulatory environment.

items.rationale: Skeptical that nominally decentralized networks may conceal trusted control points (e.g. multisigs), reflecting a concern that decentralization claims are often overstated.

Central to his scaling thesis—he details its DA economics and roadmap (EIP-4844 blobs, Danksharding) and treats it as the base layer rollups settle to, while candidly noting current throughput limits.
Repeatedly cited as a leading non-native DA layer enabling rollups to scale; central to his DA-as-bottleneck thesis.

Personally engaged and positive—'in my Solana Saga arc,' soliciting native dapp recommendations and frequently amplifying Solana figures; acknowledges in-protocol L1 sharding can be a cleaner UX path.
items.age_note: Saga enthusiasm is 2023-12-18 (~6 months old); engagement persists via 2024 technical exchanges, but no fresh explicit endorsement, treat as moderate.

items.rationale: Frames Bitcoin as the most successful 21st-century religion and first internet-native cryptonetwork worthy of constitutional/1st-Amendment-style protection—an ideological, not trading, endorsement.
Sees their committed storage capacity as candidate infrastructure for DA and for storing LLMs in decentralized-AI stacks, pending latency/erasure-coding work.
items.age_note: Filecoin-as-DA musing is 2023-07-27 (~11 months old) and exploratory ('probably a question of how to lower latency'); not restated recently—low conviction.

risk_regime.rationale: Wuollet is structurally and temperamentally bullish—an a16z accelerator lead urging founders to build decentralized networks with tokens, attack a definite future, and be 'fervently and definitely optimistic.' His latest posts radiate builder optimism (ZK underhyped, DePIN as consumer crypto's future, cypherpunks will inherit the earth) with FIT 21 read as a tailwind. Caveats are about architecture/centralization quality, not about pulling back exposure—hence risk-on rather than neutral. Note this is a builder/VC posture, not a trader's market-timing call.

notes: View is dominated by June 2024 statements, which align tightly with his 12-18 month corpus—high internal consistency, few reversals. Most-recent-weighted highlights: (1) DePIN elevated from a sector interest to his headline 'future of consumer crypto' thesis (June 2024); (2) a real reframing on tokens—from heavily promoting 'Tokenology' (Apr 2023) to insisting 'the token is not the product' and 'tokenomics is not protocol design' (June 2024), demoting tokens to incentive tools subordinate to protocol/product design (captured in age_notes). He speaks as an infrastructure/protocol thinker, so 'excited' items are overwhelmingly sectors/primitives (ZK, DA, restaking, shared sequencing, DePIN) rather than specific tradeable tokens; token entries are inferred from projects/networks he champions and carry that caveat. No explicit token-level bearishness exists in the corpus, hence tokens_concerned is empty. Many references are to private project investments (Morpho, EigenLayer, Gensyn, Aztec, NodeKit, Nebra) rather than liquid tokens, so they inform sector conviction more than token picks. Conviction scores reflect intensity/recency of his own language, not price forecasts.
